reviews.review_msg: One of the best profs--truly cares about your learning. Never having taken an ENGL course, she was extremely helpful and forthcoming. When asking for guidance, she provides extensive advice to help improve grades. Class had biweekly reading journals and 2 essays. Marking was fair though on the lower side.

Carolyn would greatly benefit from advocating and caring about the well-being of her students in her classes and the English department. I strongly recommend avoiding enrollment in any of her classes.

I finish my degree this year and looking back I can say that Lesjak was the most boring waste of time. Lectures were her opinions about the readings. She could be very unprofessional. She started class one day complaining about her teenage daughter, called her "a flake" and said "teenagers are the Devil" (I was a teenager when I took the class).

I took this class as an elective, having only taken 1 other post-secondary English course (ENGL 111W), I was a bit concerned, but Carolyn was very helpful and gave great feedback. Lectures were async, which was nice. Overall an enjoyable class. Thanks Carolyn.

Carolyn is AMAZING. Such a nice lady. 3 papers, 1 final. 5 books to read + lots of online readings and a couple films (a lot of work). She made everything so much more interesting than it seemed. TA's grade a little tough on the papers

Lesjak loves the subject matter. I loved the intertwining of critical race tenets, patriarchal relations and gendered discourse that her selection of literature entailed. However, I found her lectures dragged on, and that I learned very little, perhaps due to my lack of interest in English. Yet she was very cheerful, and I can see her appeal.

Carolyn is a super nice lady and extremely knowledgeable - but this course is way too difficult for a lower division course. Assignments were unclear and very open to interpretation - with marking that doesnt fully explain what you are doing incorrect. However, she is always available for help when asked. Make sure to participate in all lectures.

Carolyn is really nice and approachable. Very knowledgeable but not at all arrogant. She got to know everyone well. The 3-hour seminar went by much faster than all of my other 2-hour lectures... class was always interesting and thought-provoking. The reading material seemed difficult at first but with time became easier. Would definitely recommend!

Carolyn is a truly amazing professor. She cares immensely about her students and is truly passionate about what she teaches. She makes lectures interesting, is personable and often hilarious as well. She gives incredible feedback on assignments and is always willing to help you improve. I would highly recommend her if you're willing to work hard.

She is one of the friendliest profs I have met. Shes filled with passion. Only negative thing from her lectures would be that she points out way too many things; it can be hard to figure out what points are important for the exam. However, she gives really good assistance and cares for her students. Better have her as a TA also ;)

Carolyn is very passionate and enthusiastic and you can tell she throughly enjoys teaching. Lectures sometimes feel a bit rushed because there is so much to cover in so little time. She will post the powerpoint lectures but make sure you don't miss lecture because she goes into a lot more detail than what is posted on her slides.

Carolyn is incredible! I took this course as a W requirement with very little interest in studying English, but Carolyn completely changed my mind. She's cheerful, energetic and engaging. She really cares about her class and the books she uses - and she gives out tons of great essay topics in class, if you're listening! Kind person and great prof.

Carolyn is great! She makes lectures fun with her goofy sense of humour, and is very passionate about English. She is not overly strict, and definitely supports free thinking! Class expectations were clear and marking was very fair. I just really enjoyed every lecture with Carolyn, and would definitely recommend her!

Carolyn is great!! Loved the course, and she makes it fun by showing us goofy music videos and joking around once and a while. Expectations were clear and marking was fair. Really knows her stuff. Would definitely recommend!
Best English class I have ever taken. Every lecture seemed to actually have a purpose. She made her ideas and expectations fairly clear throughout the entire class .Interesting and thought provoking.
Literary Criticism = boring, at least to me. Thank goodness Carolyn was my Prof. She made the course tolerable with her great personality and her teaching style.

Carolyn is amazing! While I agree the 1000 page book was a bit much, she did take our complaints seriously and I think she may change this in the future. She's warm and friendly and super-freaking smart. She will help you as much as you need her to with your papers, and she genuinely wants you to do well... and LEARN! Do not miss out on this prof!

She's unreasonable.  Who assigns a 1000 page novel in the middle of a semester where midterms are likeliest?  She doesn't stick to her own outline, has little respect for class end times (we rarely get breaks and classes always seem to end at 11:30 not 11:20 as they should).
Probably my favourite professor at SFU so far
She's a great prof. encourages free thinking and interpretation rather than telling you what to think. She's really nice, lectures were fun and content was really interesting.

The course material she chose was generally awful and her mid term and final were way too long...however...Dr Lesjak is a lovely person who has a great attitude and seems to know what she's talking about. She's a bit more political than most other English profs, so keep that in mind.

Great prof! Take her courses she's the best at what she does and is a lot of fun. She's kind and extremely positive and is very flexible. Lots of xtra reading material, but interesting stuff. Movies in class, long presentations, serious marker (make sure ur essays are in tip top shape-send her ur thesis if ur not sure). A really wonderful person.

It was a pleasure to take study Victorian literature with Dr. Lesjak.  Course material was delivered with energy, and readings were varied to challenge/suit most tastes.  Dr. Lesjak was very approachable.  She responded positively to questions, and provided copious and constructive comments on essays.  Hope to take more courses with her.

New to SFU's faculty and the Victorian Era team (previously just Linley and Colligan) and definitely an asset to the department. Very very helpful and committed to helping students really understand the material and write more effectively. Collaborates with students, not fixed and rigid in mindset.
